prose: Fukui reactivity analysis of benzaldehyde (14 atoms) was performed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (PySCF RKS, tier 'standard'), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Fukui functions were computed via finite-difference of Mulliken partial charges across the neutral (N), cationic (N−1, charge +1, mult 2), and anionic (N+1, charge −1, mult 2) states. All three SCF runs converged. Integrity status: trustworthy=true; fukui charge conservation: sum(f+)=1.0000, sum(f-)=1.0000. The most electrophilic site is atom 3 (C, ipso ring carbon) with f+ = 0.39938965. The most nucleophilic site is atom 1 (O, carbonyl oxygen) with f- = 0.23839906. No engine warnings were reported.
